name: Fan-out + Native Probes Demo
description: Showcases v1.18.2 native commands on cluster members. The IIS tier has 8 nodes; instead of duplicating the same /health URL 8 times, the parent declares a SINGLE `check_native` with `{hostname}` placeholders and the agent templates it per member at run-time. Two members override the parent (`check_native_override`) to demo the precedence rule: per-member override wins, otherwise the parent's native is templated, otherwise the shell fallback is used. No installer needed — every probe is HTTP/TCP from the agent's own reqwest/tokio runtime.

components.name: IIS Tier
components.display_name: IIS Pool (8 members, native HTTP probes)
components.// templating: The parent's `check_native.url` contains `{hostname}`. The agent substitutes it with each member's own `hostname` field at dispatch time, so a single declaration here probes 8 different URLs. `{install_path}` is also supported for native fields that need it. Bearer tokens go on the parent and are reused per member; they're masked in audit logs (Debug impl).

cluster_members.// override_explanation: This member exposes /healthz instead of /health (legacy build), so it carries its own `check_native_override`. Per-member override wins over the templated parent.
And this one is on a non-standard port. `check_native_override` again — fully replaces the parent's templated probe.

notes.precedence: Per slot (check / start / stop), the agent picks: 1) member.*_native_override, 2) parent.*_native templated for this member, 3) member.*_cmd_override (shell), 4) parent.*_cmd (shell).
notes.templating: Recognised placeholders: `{hostname}` (always set), `{install_path}` (empty string if the member has none). Substituted in HTTP url/headers/body/expect_body_contains and TCP host. Anything else (incl. stray `{}`) is left untouched.
notes.audit: `bearer_token` and Authorization/X-API-Key/Cookie headers are masked in tracing output and audit details — search for `***` in your log aggregator to verify.
